marketState: neutral, bullish undercurrents (weather/COT/seasonality) fighting a bearish storage/trend backdrop
summary: Front price ($2.928) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.919) today, so there is no near-term mispricing edge, but the fair-value curve rises to $3.012 by +21D — consistent with the +3.7% typical 4-week seasonal move and Sep being historically the strongest month. The 8-16 day weather outlook is extreme (+45.7% demand vs 5y, above the top of its historical range) and managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of five years, a classic contrarian setup that argues for upside risk into peak cooling season. Working against that: storage is +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise this week, and every technical timeframe from 5-minute to weekly still shows a 50<200 EMA bearish structure, with the daily/weekly trend outright bearish even as 5m/15m readings are overbought (RSI 73, price above the upper Bollinger band) from a recent bounce. Seasonality reliability is explicitly flagged Low (63pp 10-90 spread), so the seasonal tailwind should be sized cautiously. Net effect: medium-term fundamentals/positioning lean long, near-term price action looks stretched and due for a pause or pullback within the still-bearish trend structure. Note: production data was not present in this snapshot, so supply-side context is incomplete.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +45.7% vs 5y and above the top of its historical range, managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of 5 years (contrarian bullish/short-covering risk), and the fair-value curve climbs from $2.92 now to $3.01 by +21D as the market enters Sep, seasonally the strongest month. Storage +183 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise caps how far this can run and argues for moderate, not high, conviction.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained close below ~$2.70, the 8-16D heat forecast normalizing back toward the 5y average, or continued weekly storage builds surprising bearish (confirming the oversupply narrative over the weather narrative)

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (low conviction, countertrend fade)
shortTerm.rationale: Front price is already at fair value (spread +$0.009, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, while 5m/15m technicals are overbought (RSI 73/67.6, price above the upper Bollinger band) after a bounce inside a daily/weekly downtrend where every timeframe still shows a 50<200 EMA bearish structure. Near-term weather (1-7D) is only modestly above normal (+2.7% vs 5y, mid-range), not enough to override the overbought/technical pullback risk.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close above $3.05, or 1-7D weather demand accelerating sharply above normal, which would confirm the bounce instead of a fade
